frictions.description: CLI and daemon communicate via Unix socket but daemon fails to read commands
frictions.resolution: Root cause identified in server.rs: handle_connection() uses `vec![0u8; 4096]` which pre-initializes buffer with 4096 zeros. Tokio's read_buf() appends to buffer but length==capacity so no data is actually read. The daemon receives empty input and fails to parse as JSON.

blockers.description: Daemon socket reading bug: server.rs line 54 initializes buffer with `vec![0u8; 4096]` creating a Vec with 4096 bytes already used. Tokio's read_buf() appends to this buffer but cannot because length==capacity. Result: daemon reads 0 bytes and tries to parse empty string as JSON, getting 'expected value at line 1 column 1' error.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested with Python socket client - even sending valid JSON like '[]' or '{}' returns the same error. This confirms the issue is in the daemon's read implementation, not the CLI's JSON serialization.

summary: All 4 CLI assertions (VAL-CLI-001 through VAL-CLI-004) are blocked due to a fundamental socket reading bug in the daemon. The issue is in crates/swell-daemon/src/server.rs at line 54: the buffer is initialized with `vec![0u8; 4096]` which pre-fills it with 4096 zero bytes. When tokio's read_buf() tries to append socket data to this buffer, it cannot because the buffer appears full (length == capacity == 4096). The daemon effectively reads 0 bytes and then fails to parse an empty string as JSON. This affects all CLI commands since they all depend on the socket communication working correctly.
